text:
Market today: Mood is cautious — Wall Street clustering around a single S&P target suggests consensus, not conviction. Chips and tech are drifting, no clear catalyst today. Your margin exposure makes any drift feel worse than it looks.

Your holdings: PLTR is the loudest problem — down 12.77% and flagged SELL; on margin that hole compounds fast, stop at 85 gives more room but the signal says act. AVGO is your largest position, down 12.45% and flagged TRIM — size plus loss plus margin is a dangerous combo, consider cutting exposure now. SPAL is down 14.48% in a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily — it's burning even in a flat market; stop at 19 is close, watch it today. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history — keep it small and manage it manually.

Watch: 1) SK Hynix news — any ripple into chip sentiment hits SMH and AVGO directly. 2) Broader tech tone around the SpaceX/AI narrative — negative press could pressure SPCX and SPAL fast.

Bottom line: Your two biggest losers are also your two biggest risks on margin — PLTR and AVGO need a decision today, not tomorrow.
